§6PVC§f

§aCadmium-based plastic alternative — easier than hard plastic but requires careful material management!§f

PVC (Polyvinylchloride) is an alternative plastic to Hard Plastic, produced from §bUnsaturated Hydrocarbons§f, §bChlorine Gas§f, and §bCadmium Powder§f in the §bChemical Plant§f. It is easier to produce than Hard Plastic since it does not require BTX, but introduces cadmium into your production chain.

§6Production§f:
§b• Chemical Plant§f: §e250mB§f Unsaturated Hydrocarbons + §e250mB§f Chlorine Gas + Cadmium Powder → §e2×§f PVC Bar (requires §bPolyvinylchloride Synthesis§f template).

§6Uses§f:
§b• Machinery§f: PVC replaces Hard Plastic in many recipes where applicable.
§b• Pyrolysis Oven§f: PVC is used directly in the Pyrolysis Oven recipe.

§eTip§f: Compared to Hard Plastic, PVC uses §e50%§f less Unsaturated Hydrocarbons and §e50%§f less Chlorine Gas per bar, and produces §e2×§f the output per batch. However, it requires §bCadmium§f, which must be mined or processed separately. Choose PVC over Hard Plastic when cadmium is abundant and BTX is scarce!
